title: Static and Media Files
content: ### Static Files Configuration Static files include CSS, JavaScript, and images. Configure them in `settings.py`: ```python # Static files (CSS, JavaScript, images) STATIC_URL = '/static/' # Additional directories for collectstatic to look for static files STATICFILES_DIRS = [ BASE_DIR / 'static', # project-level static folder ] # Where collectstatic places all files for production STATIC_ROOT = BASE_DIR / 'staticfiles' ``` ### Using Static Files in Templates Load the `static` template tag: ```html {% load static %} ``` ### Collectstatic In production, `collectstatic` gathers all static files from apps and `STATICFILES_DIRS` into `STATIC_ROOT`: ```bash python manage.py collectstatic ``` This makes it easy to serve all static files from a single directory. ### Organizing Static Files Per-app static files are automatically discovered: ``` myapp/ static/ myapp/ css/ app.css js/ app.js images/ icon.png ``` Always namespace your static files inside a subdirectory named after the app to avoid conflicts. ### Media Files Media files are user-uploaded content (avatars, images, documents). Configure in `settings.py`: ```python # URL that serves media files MEDIA_URL = '/media/' # Filesystem path for uploaded files MEDIA_ROOT = BASE_DIR / 'media' ``` ### Serving Media Files During Development In `urls.py`, add media serving (only for development): ```python from django.conf import settings from django.conf.urls.static import static urlpatterns = [ # ... your URL patterns ] if settings.DEBUG: urlpatterns += static(settings.MEDIA_URL, document_root=settings.MEDIA_ROOT) ``` ### File Fields in Models ```python from django.db import models def user_avatar_path(instance, filename): return f'avatars/user_{instance.user.id}/{filename}' class Profile(models.Model): user = models.OneToOneField(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE) avatar = models.ImageField(upload_to=user_avatar_path, blank=True) resume = models.FileField(upload_to='resumes/', blank=True) ``` ### In Templates ```html {% load static %} ``` ### Production Deployment For production, use dedicated serving: - **Static files**: Serve via Nginx, Apache, or WhiteNoise - **Media files**: Serve via Nginx or cloud storage (S3, Google Cloud Storage) ```nginx # Nginx configuration location /static/ { alias /path/to/staticfiles/; } location /media/ { alias /path/to/media/; } ```
codeExample: {% load static %} {{ title }}

quiz.question: What does the 'python manage.py collectstatic' command do?
options: Gathers all static files into a single directory (STATIC_ROOT)

quiz.question: Which setting defines the base URL for serving user-uploaded files?
